evals.name: backend-selection
evals.prompt: We're standing up NVIDIA Dynamo for disaggregated prefill on AWS EC2 with EFA NICs (p5.48xlarge instances, 8x H100 each, multi-node). Need to pick a NIXL backend for the prefill→decode KV transfer. Which backend is the right choice for EFA, what NIXL env vars must I set in the pod spec, and what gotchas should I know about (especially anything fixed in recent NIXL releases that I should make sure I'm on)?

assertions.text: Recommends libfabric as the primary backend for EFA NICs (validated upstream)
Mentions UCX as a possible alternative or fallback for EFA
Names NIXL_PLUGIN_DIR as a relevant environment variable
Mentions ETCD (NIXL_ETCD_ENDPOINTS) as the metadata mode of choice for multi-node elastic clusters
Calls out NIXL >= v1.0.1 specifically because of libfabric thread-safety / notif-on-repost fixes
Lists hwloc as a libfabric backend dependency
Lists libnuma as a libfabric backend dependency
Pod IP via downward API (status.podIP) for the side-channel host
Mentions headless Service (clusterIP: None) requirement for pod-to-pod NIXL handshake
Mentions NIXL_ERR_REMOTE_DISCONNECT first-request handshake race (expected, retry second)
Provides a concrete K8s YAML env: section snippet
Notes UCX_TLS is not the relevant variable for libfabric (or alternatively if UCX recommended, includes UCX_TLS=cuda_copy,sm,tcp)

evals.name: python-example
evals.prompt: Write me a minimal complete Python NIXL example showing two processes on the same machine doing a GPU tensor READ between them. I want correct setup of the listener thread, register_memory, metadata exchange, transfer, completion check, and teardown. Use the official Python API from the nixl package (pip install nixl). Single file, self-contained, runnable.

assertions.text: Imports nixl_agent and nixl_agent_config from the nixl package (not from internal _api / _bindings)
Uses nixl_agent_config with enable_listen_thread=True at least on the target side
Allocates a torch tensor on a CUDA device (e.g. cuda:0 or cuda:1)
Calls register_memory on the tensor
Calls get_xfer_descs to build descriptor list
Initiator calls fetch_remote_metadata and/or send_local_metadata (with ip, port for socket mode)
Calls initialize_xfer with operation argument 'READ' (or alternatively prep_xfer_dlist + make_prepped_xfer with READ)
Calls transfer() and polls check_xfer_state for DONE / handles ERR
Includes teardown: deregister_memory and remove_remote_agent (and ideally invalidate_local_metadata, release_xfer_handle)
Single self-contained file with mode/role argument or two-side branching
Comment block at top explaining how to launch (target first, then initiator)
Code is runnable as-is — no obvious syntax errors, missing imports, or undefined variables

evals.name: debug-segfault
evals.prompt: Our vLLM disagg-prefill prefiller pod keeps segfaulting after the first prefill. Stack trace shows `nixlUcxSharedThread::run()` and there's a UCX warning in the logs: `W ucx_utils.cpp:581: memory is detected as host`. We're running NixlConnector on Kubernetes, the image is vllm/vllm-openai:cu130-nightly with INSTALL_KV_CONNECTORS=true. Our pod env currently has UCX_TLS=tcp and we set VLLM_NIXL_SIDE_CHANNEL_HOST to the Service FQDN. What's wrong and exactly what do I change?

assertions.text: Identifies UCX_TLS=tcp alone as the segfault root cause
Recommends UCX_TLS=cuda_copy,sm,tcp (or extended cuda_copy,cuda_ipc,sm,tcp,rc) as the fix
Connects 'memory is detected as host' UCX warning to the missing cuda_copy transport
Identifies VLLM_NIXL_SIDE_CHANNEL_HOST set to Service FQDN as the second bug
Recommends pod IP via downward API (fieldRef: status.podIP) as the fix
Mentions headless Service requirement (clusterIP: None) for cross-pod NIXL
Provides a concrete corrected K8s YAML env: section
Notes NIXL_ERR_REMOTE_DISCONNECT on first request as a separate, expected handshake-race issue
Does not propose unrelated wild-goose fixes (e.g., increasing GPU memory, restarting cluster, changing model)
